summary: I reviewed the technical design (`design.md`) against the business requirements (`requirements.md`, R1–R18) and against the seven findings from iteration 1. All seven prior findings are resolved: the design now suppresses the iteration number on first attempts (R4), names both steps when two are working (R5), collapses to "<Phase>: N steps are running" for three or more (R6), gives a distinct failed-state branch that names where it stopped (R9), adds an aborted→"Canceled" / future paused→"Paused" branch (R11), makes `build` a total function with an unconditional catch-all (R12), and removes the out-of-scope `as_of` timestamp. My fresh full pass confirms every requirement R1–R18 maps to a concrete component and nothing is designed that no requirement asks for (the extra Blocked/stuck branch is a real state truthfully covered under R12, not scope creep). I emitted a **pass** verdict with an empty findings list to `verdict.json`.
